sections.paragraph: (“a fortified place,” “a castle,” for שְׁדֹד l. c. from שָׁדַד), Ashdod, Ἀζωτός, pr.n. one of the five chief cities of the Philistines (although assigned to the tribe of Judah, Jos 15:4), Jos 11:22 15:46 1Sa 5:1 Isa 20:1. It was the fortress of Palestine, on the borders of Palestine and Egypt, compare Isa. l. cit. and Herod. ii. 157. There still exists the village Esdûd or Atzud. See Rosenm. Alterthumskunde, ii. 2, page 374, seq. The Gentile noun is אַשְׁדֹּודִי, fem. ־ִית, and the latter as an adv. in the tongue or dialect of Ashdod, Neh 13:23.
